name: Penn & Teller VR: F U, U, U, & U

reviews.review_description: I've written support tickets and have yet to receive a response.... For now save your money and don't buy becsuse without the companion app, 90% of the tricks are unplayable. With the app working, I'm sure this game would be 5 stars....

The companion web app now works and the game is really funny!
It's great to be a "magician" with some physic effects that will surprise your friends.
Sometime a little bit verbose.
P.S. If the app does not connect, please uninstall and re install it. It worked for me.

Just worst game  ever ...I felt some type of way .
I.j.s

If you’re looking for a fun experience to prank your friends then this is for you!

I love Penn & Teller. They always have a fun wrinkle in their work which brings it above the rest. This app fits the pattern, placing you at the center of a magic show, while they dispel the “magic”.  It’s a very polished experience. 

Two thirds of the experiences are “unfair” tricks you can play on your friends. And they are unfair, often relying on them having a VR blindfold over their face to allow you to pull these off. But they’re fun and varied and are much longer than I expected. It will take a good hour or so just to learn your part in all of the tricks, though it seems they have a companion app to help I need to check out. 

One third of the bits are anti-jokes. An example is their “American Cups and Balls” performance recreated painstakingly in CGI. What’s so magic about this is that it lacks magic. It’s two craftsman that worked endlessly hard to make something feel seemless. You expect them to make it have some silly twist because it’s in VR, but no. It is as plain and awesome as reality. A table. Cups. Balls. 

You can play these experiences on your own, but most of their fun is missing. That’s my only real complaint, but I think that’s also the fun. Knowing what goes on behind the scenes probably does take the edge off the joy when you’re a magician. So, this app really isn’t for you to enjoy in VR, it’s for your friends to enjoy. Your fun happens outside of VR as you play these tricks to anyone who will put on a VR HMD on around you. 

This app really is something unique and quite special. Well done!
I don’t know why oculus has a diss as the first review. Thing works fine. Everything is as it should be. Maybe the dude who wrote the neg is a rube?

reviews.review_title: Without the app it's half a game.
reviews.review_description: As a big P&T fan I wanted this but saw the reviews about app problems. I decided to go for it and the bits you can use are great, but without the app 6 of the 13 bits are unusable. Which is a shame as they look like fun tricks. Gearbox Software don't even bother to reply when you ask about the app. If it was fully functioning this would be an easy 5 stars.
but need fix little glitches and add subtitle

We were very excited about this game when it was first revealed, Penn and Teller are legendary magicians by this point who have inspired countless others all around the world to get into magic. The idea of the game is to learn tricks and then act them out to your friends and family. The absolutely cringey game name (that you can tell the people involved find genuinely hilarious) stands for “Frankly Unfair, Unkind, Unnecessary, & Underhanded”, but the only U we really find is relevant here is the third one.

While the concept is a great idea and there is more than enough content to justify the price, we can’t help but feel like there was a lot of missed opportunities to come up with magic tricks that would actually amaze those you perform them with. Instead, it feels like mostly pranks aimed to scare or make a fool of people, to the point where it actually asks you to grab your friend very suddenly while they can see a shotgun aimed at them, we kind of found ourselves wondering when the lowkey bullying would end and the magic would begin, we sadly didn’t end up that lucky.

While we’re big fans of Penn and Teller as entertainers, we couldn’t help but notice how much Penn loves the sound of his own voice, babbling on far more than is necessary to teach you the tricks. If you bought this shortly before having family or friends over, they’d have to sit around for 30-60 minutes while you wade through endless dialogue and tutorials just to learn a very simple trick. This isn’t a show Penn and Teller, it’s a video game, a video game that’s potential was sawed in half.

Since our review was published the official (and essential) companion app has also stopped working and seemingly nothing is being done to fix it, so we're dropping our review by a further star - typical Gearbox.

Read the full review and more here: https://www.opiumpulses.com/article/411/10-more-oculus-quest-game-reviews-p2

Ok, I'm a magician and I've always wanted to share the stage with Penn and Teller. VR Is (quite simply) AMAZING. You know that. If you have a passing interest in magic you need to give this a game a look. Even if you DON'T like magic this is funny, and an opportunity to fool people in a really cool way. Enough said.
This game is unusable with out the companion app and that app is broken, plus oculus refused to refund the game￼
This app is so bad and it cost me every last bit of my money I thought u would learn magic tricks but it’s broken and doesn’t work! DONT BUY

I, like several others, have found this to be a less than stellar experience as the companion app is unusable. I bought this game right before Thanksgiving. Not once has the companion app been accessable. I just get a broken link page. I raised a support ticket a few days ago and it's still unusable.
They should just let you stream to your device but no they want you to use the companion app that doesn't even work.
I thought it would be like a behind the scenes but it’s just a how to do it sort of thing for twenty dollars when I could search it up on YouTube

Tried it last night on my unsuspecting wife, her reaction was priceless! Boy was she upset that I got her, with the help of Penn and Teller. 

A great mix of pranks to choose from. Graphics and scenes are pretty good. Companion app via website is super easy to use. They also give you detailed instructions on how to complete each ruse. 

Looking forward to pranking some more unsuspecting friends and family with this. Highly recommend if you are a fan of Penn and Teller and enjoy their humour.

I was very interested in this game, as it seemed fun and interesting. And it is, but I have had to refund because the companion app is broken. This would have been 1 star, but a few of the games worked, and were still fun, so there.
I'm not sure how the quest store still has this thing on it.   App cant' be used wthout it.

Went to setup the companion app and it's not even available?  It takes me to a link on Penn and Tellers VR page and says "login to your Oculus account" then it just takes me to my profile or says the link no longer exists.  DO NOT BUY THIS GAME.  It just doesnt work.  Not sure why it's even still available.

A critical component of the pranks of this game is the companion app, which is really a website ptvr.gearvoxsoftware.com. To connect it wants you to login/link your Oculus account but apparently the developers abandoned this critical functionality, which may have allowed you to login at one point but no longer. Regretfully refunding until they fix this, as the app looked fun and my family loves Penn & Teller.
Funny game that exploits vr to play pranks on your friends. One of the more unique titles for the Quest, brought to you by some excellent magic boys.
